summary: Russian forces launched a coordinated strike with Iskander-M ballistic missiles and hundreds of attack drones, hitting industrial sites in Kremenchuk, the Odesa region and Zaporizhzhia, while Ukrainian air defenses downed most of the incoming threats. Ukrainian special forces struck Russian logistical vessels in occupied Crimea and damaged a S‑400 radar complex. Both sides continued intensive UAV activity across multiple fronts, with numerous reported Shahed attacks on Kremenchuk and other locations.
key_points: Russian forces fired seven Iskander‑M ballistic missiles and 189 attack drones, striking Kremenchuk industrial facilities, causing a partial blackout, and hitting the Odesa region, resulting in one civilian casualty (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted three ballistic missiles and 174 of the 189 drones launched in the same attack (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Ukrainian special forces damaged Russian supply ships "Volga" and "Vyatka" and a ferry in occupied Crimea, and struck a S‑400 radar complex covering the Kerch Strait (Source: @UkraineNow/82191).
Shahed drones attacked Kremenchuk, with reports of at least four rockets and more than 20 Shahed strikes targeting the city and its hydro‑electric plant (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68070).
A Ukrainian drone strike hit the Russian chemical plant "Azot" in Novomoskovsk, also damaging a power line and a residential building (Source: @UkraineNow/82184).
FPV drones destroyed a Ukrainian ammunition depot in Sumy region, according to Russian defence ministry footage (Source: @tass_agency/382472).
Su‑34 aircraft bombed Ukrainian positions in three districts, as reported by the Russian Ministry of Defence (Source: @tass_agency/382491).

Multiple UAVs were observed over Chernihiv, Kharkiv and Poltava regions, heading toward strategic directions such as the Belarus border and local towns (Source: @kpszsu/66391, @kpszsu/66438, @eRadarrua/98232).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces executed a large‑scale missile and drone barrage, employing Iskander‑M ballistic missiles and hundreds of attack drones that struck key industrial and civilian infrastructure in Kremenchuk, Odesa and Zaporizhzhia, while Ukrainian air defenses shot down the majority of the threats. Ukrainian special operations successfully hit Russian logistical vessels in occupied Crimea and damaged a S‑400 radar complex, demonstrating continued offensive capability. Both sides maintain high‑tempo UAV operations across multiple fronts, indicating an escalating conflict environment.

points: Ukrainian air defence units intercepted three of the seven Iskander‑M missiles and 174 of the 189 attack drones launched by Russia, limiting damage to industrial sites in Kremenchuk and the Odesa region (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).

Ukrainian special forces conducted a maritime raid in occupied Crimea, damaging the Russian supply ships "Volga" and "Vyatka" and a ferry, and striking a S‑400 radar installation covering the Kerch Strait (Source: @UkraineNow/82191).

A Ukrainian UAV attacked the Russian chemical plant "Azot" in Novomoskovsk, causing damage to the plant, a power line and a residential building, with a civilian woman reportedly receiving medical aid (Source: @UkraineNow/82184).

Shahed drone attacks on Kremenchuk were reported, including at least four ballistic rockets and more than 20 Shahed strikes targeting the city and its hydro‑electric power station (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68070).

Ukrainian forces reportedly used a Shahed‑type UAV to strike a BM‑21 "Grad" launcher near the village of Snagost, confirming active use of captured or locally produced loitering munitions (Source: @DeepStateUA/23598).
Ukrainian units engaged Russian forces on the Kinburn Spit, with claims of forcing enemy withdrawal after heavy fire, though independent confirmation is lacking (Source: @UkraineNow/82175).
Ukrainian intelligence reported a UAV‑guided attack on a Russian ammunition depot in Sumy region, destroying stored munitions (Source: @tass_agency/382472).

Russian forces launched seven Iskander‑M ballistic missiles and 189 attack drones, targeting industrial infrastructure in Kremenchuk, the Odesa region and Zaporizhzhia, resulting in one civilian death and multiple infrastructure outages (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Su‑34 strike aircraft bombed Ukrainian positions in three districts, as confirmed by the Russian Ministry of Defence (Source: @tass_agency/382491).
Russian FPV drones destroyed a Ukrainian ammunition depot in Sumy region, according to video released by the Russian defence ministry (Source: @tass_agency/382472).

Russian naval assets in Crimea, including the vessels "Volga" and "Vyatka", were targeted by Ukrainian special forces, indicating heightened vulnerability of Russian supply lines (Source: @UkraineNow/82191).
Russian forces reported a mass UAV attack, described as the largest in a year, suggesting increased reliance on drone swarms for saturation attacks (Source: @tass_agency/382468).
Russian air defence units claimed to have shot down 19 FPV drones heading toward Moscow, reflecting defensive measures against Ukrainian drone incursions (Source: @tass_agency/382488).
Russian statements indicated sufficient fuel reserves for continued operations, though no direct operational detail was provided (Source: @tass_agency/382473).
Ukrainian Shahed drones were observed heading west from the Kharkiv region, indicating ongoing loitering munition activity against Russian rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/66391).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted strikes in the Donetsk area, with reports of guided bomb releases from tactical aviation targeting enemy positions (Source: @kpszsu/66467).
Russian FPV drones destroyed a Ukrainian ammunition depot in Sumy, a key logistics hub on the eastern front (Source: @tass_agency/382472).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed strike on a BM‑21 "Grad" launcher near Snagost, demonstrating continued artillery duels in the east (Source: @DeepStateUA/23598).
Multiple UAVs were tracked moving from Belgorod into the Oryshkiv district of Sumy, suggesting cross‑border reconnaissance missions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68038).
Ukrainian air defence intercepted a significant number of attack drones over the eastern sector, limiting Russian aerial pressure (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs over Donetsk and Kharkiv regions, indicating sustained offensive air operations (Source: @kpszsu/66465).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over Chernihiv region heading toward Nizhyn, indicating surveillance or strike missions near the Belarusian border (Source: @kpszsu/66391).
A group of UAVs was observed moving along the northern Chernihiv border with Belarus, heading westward (Source: @kpszsu/66438).
Ukrainian intelligence noted a reconnaissance UAV operating near Poltava heading toward Mirgorod, suggesting monitoring of supply routes (Source: @eRadarrua/98228).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV heading from the sea toward Tatarbunary in the Odesa region, indicating maritime‑to‑land surveillance (Source: @eRadarrua/98224).
Russian air defence claimed to have shot down 19 FPV drones targeting Moscow, reflecting high‑altitude UAV activity possibly originating from northern sectors (Source: @tass_agency/382488).
Ukrainian special forces conducted a maritime raid in occupied Crimea, a northern Black Sea operation (Source: @UkraineNow/82191).
Ukrainian air raid alert was lifted in Kyiv at 03:14, showing continued air threat management in the north (Source: @eRadarrua/98226).
Shahed drones attacked Kremenchuk and its hydro‑electric plant, causing damage and civilian casualties (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68070).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on the Russian chemical plant "Azot" in Novomoskovsk, southern Russia, damaging infrastructure and a residential building (Source: @UkraineNow/82184).
Ukrainian UAVs struck a building of the design engineering department of the Zaporizhzhia NPP and a private house, causing a fire and civilian injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31178).
In the Kherson region, UAV attacks killed a man in Novaya Zburyevka and wounded civilians in Kalanchak, indicating ongoing low‑altitude threats (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31178).
Ukrainian special forces damaged Russian logistical vessels in Crimea, a strategic point for southern supply lines (Source: @UkraineNow/82191).
Russian tactical aviation released guided bombs over the Kherson region, targeting Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/66467).
Ukrainian air defence downed three missiles and 174 drones during the large‑scale Russian strike, mitigating damage in the south (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).

Russian forces launched 189 attack drones and seven Iskander‑M missiles in a coordinated air strike, with Ukrainian air defence shooting down most drones and three missiles (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Ukrainian air defence reported downing 19 FPV drones heading toward Moscow, showing defensive actions against Russian UAV incursions (Source: @tass_agency/382488).
Su‑34 aircraft conducted bombing runs on Ukrainian targets in three districts, demonstrating continued Russian tactical air operations (Source: @tass_agency/382491).

Ukrainian UAVs carried out strikes on Russian infrastructure, including the chemical plant in Novomoskovsk and the Zaporizhzhia NPP engineering building (Source: @UkraineNow/82184, @ukr_leaks_eng/31178).
Multiple Shahed loitering munitions were observed over Kremenchuk, with at least four rockets and over 20 Shahed attacks reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68070).
Ukrainian forces used a Shahed‑type UAV to destroy a BM‑21 "Grad" launcher near Snagost, indicating use of captured drones for offensive purposes (Source: @DeepStateUA/23598).
Russian tactical aviation released guided bombs over Donetsk, Kharkiv and Kherson regions, sustaining pressure on Ukrainian front lines (Source: @kpszsu/66465).

key_developments: Russian Iskander‑M missile and drone barrage struck Kremenchuk, Odesa and Zaporizhzhia, causing infrastructure damage and one civilian death; Ukrainian air defence downed most threats (Source: @UkraineNow/82186).
Ukrainian special forces damaged Russian supply ships "Volga" and "Vyatka" and a ferry in occupied Crimea, and struck a S‑400 radar complex covering the Kerch Strait (Source: @UkraineNow/82191).
Shahed drones launched multiple attacks on Kremenchuk, including four rockets and over 20 loitering munitions, targeting the city's hydro‑electric plant (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68070).
Ukrainian UAV strike hit the Russian chemical plant "Azot" in Novomoskovsk, damaging the facility, a power line and a residential building (Source: @UkraineNow/82184).
FPV drones destroyed a Ukrainian ammunition depot in Sumy region, reducing Ukrainian logistical capacity (Source: @tass_agency/382472).
Su‑34 aircraft bombed Ukrainian positions in three districts, indicating continued Russian tactical air operations (Source: @tass_agency/382491).
Ukrainian UAVs struck the Zaporizhzhia NPP engineering building and a private house, causing fire and civilian injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31178).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces continue to employ high‑value ballistic missiles and large drone swarms to target Ukrainian industrial and civilian infrastructure, demonstrating an ability to conduct coordinated, multi‑domain strikes. Ukrainian special operations have shown capability to strike high‑value Russian maritime assets in occupied Crimea, disrupting supply lines and radar coverage. The proliferation of Shahed loitering munitions and UAVs on both sides increases the risk of rapid escalation and complicates air defence allocations across multiple fronts.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine's air defence network has proven effective in intercepting the majority of incoming missiles and drones, yet repeated attacks on critical infrastructure highlight vulnerabilities in resilience and redundancy. International partners continue to provide air defence systems, as evidenced by the high interception rates, while diplomatic efforts focus on sustaining military aid. Russian statements about fuel reserves suggest they can maintain current operational tempo, but logistical pressures may grow if Ukrainian strikes on supply vessels intensify.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian intelligence is actively tracking UAV flight paths across the northern, eastern and southern fronts, indicating a focus on both reconnaissance and strike missions. Russian use of FPV drones for depot destruction and mass UAV attacks points to a shift toward saturation tactics. The reported success of Ukrainian maritime raids in Crimea underscores the importance of securing Black Sea supply routes. Continued monitoring of Shahed deployment patterns will be critical for anticipating future attacks on energy and industrial targets.
